refactor(codegen): thread explicit type hints into collection builtins (#887)

* refactor(codegen): thread typeHint into generateBuiltinCall for Vec/HashMap/HashSet::new

Pass pendingDeclaredType.value_or(mlir::Type{}) from the call-dispatch
site in MLIRGenExpr.cpp into generateBuiltinCall, and use the explicit
typeHint parameter instead of reading/resetting the member inside the
three collection builtins (Vec::new, HashMap::new, HashSet::new).

All other pendingDeclaredType consumers (Some/Ok/Err/None/array/map/
block-literal) are unchanged. Stmt-level set/reset in MLIRGenStmt.cpp
is unchanged. This is a pure refactor slice with no semantic change.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(codegen): clear builtin type hint before sibling lowering

Capture and clear pendingDeclaredType at the builtin-call dispatch site before
delegating to generateBuiltinCall, so explicit collection constructor hints
cannot leak into sibling subexpressions evaluated during the same RHS.

Add a focused mlirgen regression test covering Vec::new() adjacent to an array
literal in the same expression.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(android): wire authenticated household ID across ViewModels (#635) (#745)

* feat(android): wire authenticated household ID across ViewModels (#635)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(android): guard test-reporter on XML existence and register TransactionDetailViewModel

- Condition dorny/test-reporter step on hashFiles() so it only runs
  when JUnit XML reports actually exist. v3.0.0 has no fail-on-empty
  input and always fails when no files match the glob, which masks the
  real compilation/test error with a misleading 'No test report files
  were found' message.

- Register TransactionDetailViewModel in AppModule via viewModelOf().
  TransactionDetailScreen.kt obtains it through koinViewModel() but
  the binding was never added, which would cause a Koin resolution
  crash at runtime when the detail route is wired in.

Fixes the 'Build & Test' CI failure on PR #745.

* fix(android): resolve Kotlin compilation errors for household ID wiring (#635)

Three AccountsViewModelTest methods constructed AccountsViewModel directly
without passing the new householdIdProvider parameter introduced in the
household-ID wiring PR. The createViewModel() helper was already updated
but these three tests bypassed it:

- selectAccount sets selected account and loads transactions
- selectAccount transactions sorted by date descending
- clearSelection resets selected account and transactions

Added householdIdProvider = TestHouseholdIdProvider() to each call site,
matching the pattern used by all other ViewModel tests.

chore(main): release 0.1.0

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Refine CI workflow: limit push triggers and gate coverage reports (#393)

* fix: enable coverage report posting to PRs

The vitest-coverage-report-action requires pull_request event context to

commit.message:
nfwd: canonical slot traversal via `_fold_slots` / `_unfold_slots` (#1136)

* Refactor nfwd: canonical slot traversal via _fold_slots / _unfold_slots

Add two traversal helpers to the Nfwd module that define the canonical
slot order exactly once:

- _fold_slots(f, init, x, state): left fold over scalar slots
  (real=1, complex=2 re/im, arrays in eachindex, tuples L→R)
- _unfold_slots(f, x, state): leaf-level structural map over
  primitive leaves (scalars, complex, arrays; tuples recurse)

Refactor existing nfwd machinery to use these helpers:

- _nfwd_input_dof: now a one-liner fold that counts slots
- _nfwd_seed_tangents: tuple recursion replaced by _unfold_slots
- _nfwd_scatter_scalar_chunk: replaces both itself and the recursive
  _nfwd_update_scalar_grad with a single _unfold_slots traversal

Add focused tests verifying the traversal contract: real/complex
scalars, dense real/complex arrays, tuple mixtures, Float32, and
explicit checks that fold and unfold visit slots in the same order.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix nfwd scrutinise findings: docstrings, DOF counting, comment

- Rewrite _fold_slots / _unfold_slots docstrings: each slot is one
  differentiable scalar DOF; complex contributes real-then-imaginary;
  tuples left to right; arrays in eachindex order
- Restore O(1) _nfwd_input_dof specialisations for IEEEFloat, Complex,
  and AbstractArray (fold-based counting was O(n) for arrays)
- Correct section comment: CUDA extension uses _nfwd_leaf_dof /
  _nfwd_slot_meta, not NfwdMooncake

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Simplify: collapse _unfold_slots leaf overloads; fix fragile test

- Collapse four identical _unfold_slots leaf overloads (IEEEFloat,
  Complex, AbstractArray real/complex) into a single Union dispatch;
  same allowlist semantics, no code duplication
- Replace sum_slots test helper with sum_slot_idx: the old helper
  reimplemented _nfwd_add_slot! slot-index conventions (cld, isodd)
  in test code; new helper sums within-leaf slot indices instead,
  testing the same callback contract without the fragile coupling

commit.message:
Move `Thread::m_pInterpThreadContext` near top of Thread class for stable asm offset (#126684)

`Thread::m_pInterpThreadContext` was declared at the very end of the
`Thread` class, making its offset highly volatile across build
configurations. Moving it towards the top of the `Thread` class to a
small set of stable, always-present fields at the top of the `Thread`
class will make it a lot more stable, and less prone to build breaks.

fix: repair redirect tester + add GET /api/links/{link_id} endpoint (#19)

## Summary

Fixes the **Redirect tester** feature in the UI, which was silently
broken due to a browser security restriction.

## Root cause

The frontend `testRedirect()` function used `fetch()` with `redirect:
'manual'`. For cross-origin requests (frontend on `:5173`, backend on
`:8000`) this produces an **opaque redirect** response — the browser
hides all headers including `Location`, and `res.status` is always `0`.
As a result, the tester always showed blank status and location.

## Changes

### Backend
- Added `GET /api/links/{link_id}` endpoint that returns `LinkOut`
metadata without triggering a redirect — a clean REST resource read

### Frontend
- `testRedirect()` now calls `GET /api/links/{link_id}` and displays
**redirect code**, **target URL**, and **active** status
- Added `getLink()` helper to `api.ts`

### Tests
- Two new integration tests for the new endpoint (200 with metadata, 404
for unknown ID)
- All 30 tests pass, linting clean

## Also on this branch
- `docs: remove rate limiting requirement` — minor docs update

commit.message:
fix: remove MI_CROSSPLANE_CLIENT_ID from pipeline, add idempotent KV secret import

- Remove MI_CROSSPLANE_CLIENT_ID loading from both tfvars-load steps — the
  variable was removed from terraform.tfvars in the previous refactor and no
  longer exists; loading it produced an empty string causing the storage role
  assignment to target nobody
- Fix state storage role assignment: change --assignee from the now-removed
  MI client ID to $servicePrincipalId (sp-crossplane's app ID, injected by
  addSpnToEnvironment) so Terraform can actually read/write blobs in the
  state storage account
- Add 'Import Existing KV Secrets (idempotent)' step between Terraform Init
  and Terraform Plan: for each of the 6 KV secrets, checks if the resource
  is already in state (skip) or exists in Azure but not in state (import).
  Computes the vault name using the same formula as Terraform local.kv_name.
  First run: vault/secrets don't exist — all created normally.
  Re-run or state drift: secrets imported before plan — no 'already exists' errors.
